description: Unified trigger evaluation queries for all 9 maestro skills. Tests whether the correct skill fires for each prompt. Merged from cross-skill discrimination set and per-skill description-optimization sets.

evals.query: I just cloned this repo and want to set up maestro so we can start tracking features
can you scaffold the project context? I need the tech stack doc and product guidelines before we start building anything
evals.notes: Explicit scaffolding request with setup-specific outputs

evals.query: initialize maestro in this project -- product context, tech stack, guidelines, the works
let's onboard this codebase to the maestro workflow, it's a Python FastAPI backend
run maestro setup with --reset, I want to redo the product definition and tech stack
scaffold the .maestro directory with all the context files -- product, tech stack, guidelines, workflow
this is a brand new project, let's get maestro initialized before we start any feature work
configure maestro for this monorepo -- it has a React frontend in apps/web and a Go backend in services/api
I want to regenerate the AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files for this project. The codebase has changed a lot since they were last generated.
we need a context file that explains the codebase to other agents. something with the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework you use
evals.notes: WHAT/WHY/HOW is the AGENTS.md framework marker

evals.query: generate the progressive disclosure context files -- building_the_project.md, running_tests.md, that kind of thing
evals.notes: Progressive disclosure files are AGENTS.md outputs, not setup

evals.query: create CLAUDE.md for this codebase -- I want Claude to know the build commands, tech stack, and rules
I need an AGENTS.md that covers what this project is, how to build it, and the coding conventions
rebuild the AGENTS.md from scratch -- reset, the codebase has changed significantly since it was generated
create context files so any AI coding agent understands this repo -- progressive disclosure, under 100 lines
evals.notes: Agent context files with line limit

evals.query: make an AGENTS.md with the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework for this Python data pipeline project
I need to add a dark mode toggle to the settings page. Pretty straightforward -- just a boolean preference that swaps the CSS theme.
evals.notes: Simple, well-scoped feature -- new-track, not design

evals.query: let's create a track for fixing that bug where the sidebar collapses on mobile when you rotate the device. I can reproduce it on iPhone 14.
evals.notes: Bug fix track -- new-track handles bugs and simple features

evals.query: there's a login timeout bug where sessions expire after 5 minutes instead of 30, let's plan the fix
new feature: implement real-time notifications using websockets and a Redis pub/sub backend
evals.notes: Feature with clear tech choices -- scoped enough for new-track

evals.query: create a track to refactor the database connection pooling -- it's causing timeouts under load
start a new bug track for the broken file upload on mobile Safari -- files over 5MB fail silently
plan out the work for adding Stripe payment integration with subscriptions and invoicing
evals.notes: Feature planning -- scoped to single integration

evals.query: new chore: upgrade all major dependencies including React 19, Next.js 15, and TypeScript 5.5
I want to add a simple 'export to CSV' button on the reports page. just dump the table data as a csv download. maybe 20 lines of code.
create a track for updating the footer copyright year from 2025 to 2026 across all pages

ok I want to build a real-time collaborative editing system. multiple users editing the same document simultaneously with conflict resolution, presence indicators, cursor tracking, and offline support with sync. this is going to touch the websocket layer, the document model, the auth system, and we might need a CRDT library.
evals.notes: Multi-component system, many subsystems -- design territory

evals.query: we need to design the payment processing pipeline. it has to handle stripe and paypal, support subscriptions and one-time payments, deal with webhooks, handle refunds, comply with PCI-DSS, and integrate with our existing user/org model. getting this wrong would be really expensive.
evals.notes: Regulated domain, high cost of error, multi-integration

evals.query: I want to add a feature but I'm not totally sure what it should look like yet. Something around notifications -- maybe email, maybe push, maybe both? And we'd need preferences per user, quiet hours, batching... honestly I need help figuring out the requirements first.
evals.notes: Unclear requirements + multi-component -- design's sweet spot

evals.query: we're building a multi-tenant SaaS platform from scratch. need to design the tenant isolation model, billing integration, role-based access per tenant, data partitioning strategy, and onboarding flow. some tenants will be on shared infra, others need dedicated. oh and we need SOC2 compliance.
evals.notes: Maximum complexity -- clearly design, not new-track

evals.query: I want to design a plugin architecture for our editor -- third party devs should be able to write plugins with a stable API, there needs to be a sandbox for security, a marketplace for discovery, and versioning so plugins don't break on updates. this is a core platform bet for us.
let's do deep discovery on building a HIPAA-compliant patient records system with audit logging and role-based access
design an event-driven microservices architecture for our e-commerce platform -- order processing, inventory, payments, shipping
help me design a complex RBAC system with dynamic permissions, SSO via SAML/OIDC, and comprehensive audit logging
deep dive into requirements for a financial transaction processing system with reconciliation, compliance reporting, and fraud detection
let's do thorough discovery for an AI-powered content moderation pipeline -- classification, appeals workflow, human review queue, policy engine
ok the plan for the auth-refactor track looks good. let's start implementing it. single agent is fine.
run the dark-mode track with --team, I want parallel agents on it since the phases are independent
resume implementing the dashboard track -- I left off partway through phase 2 yesterday
I'm ready to implement -- kick off the dark_mode_20260301 track in team mode with parallel workers
execute the payment_integration track -- we've got 3 phases, use --parallel to speed it up
the spec and plan are approved, go ahead and implement the notifications feature track
pick up the bug fix track and start working through the tasks
start implementing phase 2 of the db_migration track, phase 1 is already done
let's start coding the tasks from that plan we just finished, single agent is fine
I finished implementing the search feature. can you review the code against the spec? I want to make sure I didn't miss any requirements.
do a code review on the current track -- check for security issues and make sure the tests actually cover what the spec asks for
check if the api-v2 track passes review. I think there might be some spec requirements I missed in the error handling section.
evals.notes: Review with concern about spec coverage

evals.query: review the auth_flow track implementation -- does it match the spec we wrote?
verify the payment_integration track -- check code quality, test coverage, and any security issues
I finished implementing the user_settings track, can you audit the code against the spec?
review what we've built for the api_refactor track, especially the breaking changes
validate the db_migration track against the plan, did we miss any tasks?
review --current, let's see how the active track holds up against the spec
what's the status of all my tracks? which ones are done, which are in progress, and what should I work on next?
how far along are we on the api-v2 track? I need to know how many tasks are left in phase 2
how are we doing on the tracks? give me an overview of everything
show me where things stand with the current implementation -- phases, tasks, blockers
what should I work on next? which track or task is up?
are there any blockers on the current tracks I should know about?
give me a status report on all maestro tracks -- completion stats and next actions
where did we leave off last session? what's the next task I should pick up?
we decided to use PostgreSQL instead of MongoDB for this project. save that as a priority note so it gets injected into every session going forward.
remember that the API rate limit is 100 req/min per user and we need to respect that in all integration tests. put it in the notepad.
show me my notepad -- what priority notes do I have saved?
make a note that we're using bun not npm for this project and it matters for CI too
I need to document a constraint: the legacy API must stay backward-compatible through v3 launch. pin this as a priority note.
make a priority note: all API responses must include X-Request-ID in headers per the compliance audit
save this as a note -- staging DB credentials rotate every 30 days, last rotated Feb 15
clear the working memory section, most of those items are from last week and resolved
jot down: the legacy /v1/users endpoint is deprecated, redirect to /v2/users by March 15
prune the notepad -- some of those working memory items are from completed tracks
add to manual notes: always run migrations with --fake-initial on first deploy to staging
the implementation of phase 3 on the search track broke everything. roll it back -- revert just that phase, keep phases 1 and 2.
undo the last task I implemented on the auth-refactor track. the approach was wrong and I want to try a different one.
the implementation is done but I want to start fresh on the whole auth track. revert everything.
undo the implementation of the auth_flow_20260228 track -- it broke the login page for SSO users
evals.notes: Track revert with specific track name

evals.query: roll back phase 2 of the current track, the database migration was wrong and corrupted the index
revert task 'add-webhook-handler' from phase 1 of the integrations track
the payment integration track caused regressions in checkout, revert the whole thing
take back all changes from track db_migration_20260301, we need to use a different migration strategy
can you look at this python script and tell me why it's throwing a KeyError on line 47? here's the traceback...
evals.notes: Generic debugging -- no maestro skill should trigger

evals.query: write me a React component that shows a sortable data table with pagination. use shadcn/ui.
evals.notes: Direct coding task -- no maestro skill needed

evals.query: I want to create a new git branch for the upcoming release and tag it as v2.1.0
evals.notes: Git operation, not a maestro track

evals.query: read the README and explain how this project works
I need to set up my project for the first time. Can you install the dependencies with npm and configure eslint?
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:setup -- generic project setup, not maestro

evals.query: review this PR for me -- check the diff and leave comments on anything that looks off
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:review -- PR review, not track review

evals.query: what's the status of the CI pipeline? are the latest builds passing?
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:status -- CI status, not track status

evals.query: revert the last commit, I pushed it by accident
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:revert -- git revert, not track revert

evals.query: I need to add a loading spinner to the submit button. that's it, nothing else.
evals.notes: Trivial change -- too small for a track

evals.query: set up the development environment for this project -- install dependencies, configure Docker, etc.
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:setup -- dev env setup, not maestro scaffolding

evals.query: write a Jest unit test for the UserService.createUser method with mocked database calls
git revert HEAD~3 -- I want to undo the last 3 commits on this branch
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:revert -- raw git, not track revert

evals.query: can you review this pull request on GitHub? here's the link: github.com/myorg/myrepo/pull/247
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:review -- GitHub PR review, not track review

evals.query: git status -- show me the working tree
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:status -- git status, not track status

evals.query: write a README.md for this project with installation instructions, usage examples, and contributing guide
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:AGENTS.md -- README, not AGENTS.md

evals.query: add a JSDoc comment to this function explaining the retry logic and backoff strategy
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:note -- code comment, not notepad

evals.query: configure ESLint, Prettier, and Husky hooks for the codebase
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:setup -- tooling config, not maestro

evals.query: how many open GitHub issues do we have in the backlog?
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:status -- GitHub issues, not track status

evals.query: help me brainstorm feature ideas for the next quarter's roadmap
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:design -- brainstorming, not spec work

evals.query: write API documentation for the /users and /orders endpoints using OpenAPI spec
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:AGENTS.md -- API docs, not agent context

evals.query: look at my submitOrder function and tell me if there are any edge cases I'm missing
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:review -- ad-hoc code review, not track review

evals.query: create a new Next.js project from scratch using create-next-app
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:setup -- project creation, not maestro scaffolding

evals.query: update the CLAUDE.md file with the new bun test command instead of npm test
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:AGENTS.md -- manual edit, not generation

evals.query: save the output of this API call to a local JSON file for reference
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:note -- file save, not notepad

evals.query: undo my last edit to server.ts, I preferred the previous version of that function
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:revert -- file edit undo, not track revert

evals.query: review the changelog entries before we cut the v2.4.0 release
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:review -- changelog review, not track review

evals.query: remove the feature flag for dark mode from the LaunchDarkly config
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:revert -- config change, not track revert

evals.query: let's plan out how to implement a user authentication system for my Django app -- I need a spec and phased breakdown
evals.notes: Cross-skill: mentions 'plan' and 'implement' but wants spec+plan creation

evals.query: plan a simple webhook receiver endpoint that validates signatures and forwards to a queue
evals.notes: Cross-skill: simple enough for new-track, not design

evals.query: I need to design a complex multi-tenant SaaS platform with per-tenant database isolation and custom domains
evals.notes: Cross-skill: mentions 'design' + complexity signals -> design not new-track

evals.query: let's do deep discovery on the HIPAA compliance requirements -- this needs thorough domain analysis
note down that we decided to use PostgreSQL instead of MySQL for the user store
evals.notes: Cross-skill: 'note down' decision -- note not setup

evals.query: I want to capture this workflow as a reusable skill for future projects
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:note -- skill creation, not notepad

evals.query: what does the product.md context file say about our target users and key features?
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:setup/status -- reading context, not creating/checking

evals.query: I want to use the maestro workflow for this repo -- where do I start? what do I need to set up?
read the existing AGENTS.md and tell me what it says about the project
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:AGENTS.md -- reading, not generating

evals.query: explain what AGENTS.md is and why I'd want one in my repo
evals.notes: Near-miss for maestro:AGENTS.md -- explanation, not generation
